title: NOC 32124 – Pharmacy Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Pharmacy Technicians under NOC 32124 are regulated healthcare professionals supporting pharmacists in medication preparation, inventory control, and patient service delivery across Canada. With increasing prescription volumes, expanding community pharmacy services, and growth in hospital pharmacy departments, this TEER 2 occupation continues to show steady demand nationwide. For qualified international professionals, NOC 32124 offers eligibility under Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs, creating a structured pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3212 – Medical technologists and techniciansNOC Code32124

duties: Pharmacy Technicians perform some or all of the following duties:
Maintain medication inventories and prescription records
Review prescriptions for completeness and accuracy
Enter patient data and prepare medications under pharmacist supervision
Compound pharmaceutical products (IV injections, ointments, creams, oral solutions)
Process prescription payments and insurance billing
Provide instructions on medical devices and drug administration aids
Assist with pharmacy scheduling and workflow coordination
employment_requirements: Completion of a two-year pharmacy technician college programOR
Completion of a two- to three-year pharmaceutics program plus supervised practical training
Successful completion of certification exams by the Pharmacy Examining Board of Canada (PEBC) is required
Registration with a provincial regulatory body is required in all provinces except Quebec
College of Pharmacists of British Columbia
Internationally educated applicants must complete credential evaluation, PEBC exams, and provincial registration before practice.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $28,600 to $57,200, depending on province, work setting, and experience.
job_outlook: Expansion of pharmacy services in community and hospital settings
Increased demand for medication management support
Professionals working in hospital and specialized pharmacy settings may experience stronger employment opportunities.
labour_market_demand: This positive gap indicates favorable employment prospects, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, and Manitoba.
Why Immigrate to Canada as a Pharmacy Technician?
Stable employment in both retail and hospital sectors
Direct PR pathways for healthcare occupations
As pharmacists take on expanded clinical roles, pharmacy technicians play an increasingly vital operational role.
pathways: If you are a qualified Pharmacy Technician, NOC 32124 may provide a pathway toward permanent residence in Canada.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your structured pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 32124.
